title: Arlong’s Rule Tightens Around Nami
scene: On pages 9–12, the story moves through Arlong Park and Nami’s dangerous position among the fish-men. Nami’s role with Arlong becomes harder to understand as loyalty, fear, and survival overlap.

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: What!? All he did was collect some weapons!

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: He's seriously going to kill someone over that much!?

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: that son of a...!

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: H-Hey, you! Come back here or you'll be killed!

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: What's the meaning of this, Arlong!?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: All of us here have loyally paid the tribute without any complaints for the past 8 years!

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: There's no way any of us have even the slightest intention of rebelliing against you!

dialogue.speaker: Genzo
dialogue.text: So let go of Gen!

dialogue.speaker: Child Nami
dialogue.text: Nojiko! Wait!

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: I've already stated that the mere act of possessing weapons is a clear intent to rebel.

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: I have no choice but to kill this man for the safety and security of my lands!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Any complaints?

dialogue.speaker: Cocoyasi villagers
dialogue.text: You wouldn't rather have me destroy this entire village, would you...?

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: know this, humans! If you lay even a finger on any one of us,

dialogue.speaker: Cocoyasi villagers
dialogue.text: Your village will be destroyed! That's final!

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: Everyone go inside your homes...!

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: If we Rebel now, then our last 8 years will come to nothing!

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: If we wanted to reject their rule by fighting to our deaths, we should've done it long ago!

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: But we all swore, did we not?

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: We swore to a fight of endurance!

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: In order to live on!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Gen...!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: But...!

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: Well said!

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: Unusual,

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: I would

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: Living is Important!

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: It is because one lives, one can enjoy things!

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: Ugh!

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: Everyone has their own designated place and position in life!

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: This man understands the futility of resisting against that fact!

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: You see, not all living things,

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: are born equal! Shaha haha!

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: Humans are best living like humans!

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: Why you...!

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: Stop it!

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: Caugh Caugh!

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: Gen... but he...!

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: Don't you dare lay a hand on them!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Yes, what he said.

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: But it's not as if he used his weapons!

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: Please don't kill him!

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: A loud bunch, aren't they. I wonder if I should take this as a sign of rebelliion...

summary: This part builds the Arlong Park conflict by showing how Nami’s choices look contradictory from outside while Arlong’s power keeps the island trapped.
